---
title: "{{< var md-01.title >}}"
subtitle: "{{< var course.long-title >}}"
author:
  - name: "{{< var author >}}"
    affiliations:
        - name: Global Health Engineering - ETH Zurich
          url: https://ghe.ethz.ch/
date: "{{< var course.start-date >}}"
date-format: long
footer: "[{{< fa link >}} {{< var course.site-short >}}]({{< var course.site >}})"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: ../style/slides.scss
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
editor: source
execute:
  freeze: auto
  echo: true
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
library(knitr)
library(tidyverse)
library(countdown)
```

# B1: Welcome, concept-map intro, baseline map {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Slides

<!-- Filled by issue #13: welcome, expectations, motivation, concept-map intro -->

::: {.notes}
B1 runs 10:00-10:30. Filled by issue #13.
:::

## Your turn

<!-- Filled by issue #13: baseline concept-map exercise -->

```{r}
#| echo: false
countdown(minutes = 8)
```

::: {.notes}
Bounded regroup: 2 minutes, then park-and-pair; 1:1 fix at the next break.
:::

# B2: Create and clone (website repo) {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Live drawing: local and remote

[Projector goes to sleep.]{.highlight-yellow} We draw this one by hand.

::: {.notes}
Live drawing 1 (local and remote): two boxes, clone first, then push and pull. Under 3 minutes. Fallback: the export in the appendix of the handbook, images/drawing-1-local-remote.png.
:::

## My turn

<!-- Filled by issue #14: create and clone demo script -->

## Your turn

<!-- Filled by issue #14: learners create and clone their website repo -->

```{r}
#| echo: false
countdown(minutes = 10)
```

::: {.notes}
Bounded regroup: 2 minutes, then park-and-pair; 1:1 fix at the next break.
:::

## Take a break

[Please get up and move!]{.highlight-yellow} Leave your emails alone. We continue at 11:15.

![](images/pixel-art-tree.png){width="50%" fig-alt="Pixel art of a small character resting under a large leafy tree on a green hill, next to a gentle stream under a clear blue sky."}

```{r}
#| echo: false
countdown(minutes = 15)
```

::: footer
Image generated with [DALL-E 3 by OpenAI](https://openai.com/blog/dall-e/)
:::

# B3: The commit cycle (website repo) {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Live drawing: the commit cycle

[Projector goes to sleep.]{.highlight-yellow} We draw this one by hand.

::: {.notes}
Live drawing 2 (commit cycle): working files, stage, commit as labelled snapshot on the history line, push. Leave room for the history line to grow. Under 3 minutes. Fallback: images/drawing-2-commit-cycle.png.
:::

## My turn

<!-- Filled by issue #15: commit cycle, revert demo, credentials moment -->

## Your turn

<!-- Filled by issue #15: learners run the full pull-stage-commit-push cycle -->

```{r}
#| echo: false
countdown(minutes = 10)
```

::: {.notes}
Bounded regroup: 2 minutes, then park-and-pair; 1:1 fix at the next break.
:::

# B4: Collaboration cliffhanger {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Slides

<!-- Filled by issue #16: pairing slide -->

## Your turn

<!-- Filled by issue #16: clone the partner's repo, hit the push wall -->

```{r}
#| echo: false
countdown(minutes = 10)
```

::: {.notes}
Bounded regroup: 2 minutes, then park-and-pair; 1:1 fix at lunch.
:::

## Lunch

<!-- Filled by issue #16: debrief and lunch slide with the hard 12:55 return -->

# B5: Branch (manuscript repo) {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Live drawing: branching

[Projector goes to sleep.]{.highlight-yellow} We draw this one by hand.

::: {.notes}
Live drawing 3 (branching): the main line splits, commits land on the branch, the merge point stays empty until drawing 4. Under 3 minutes. Fallback: images/drawing-3-branching.png.
:::

## My turn

<!-- Filled by issue #17: branch demo on the manuscript repo -->

## Your turn

<!-- Filled by issue #17: learners branch on their own manuscript repo -->

```{r}
#| echo: false
countdown(minutes = 10)
```

::: {.notes}
Bounded regroup: 2 minutes, then park-and-pair. Regroup check before B6: is your branch pushed? Thumbs up.
:::

# B6: Pull request, review, merge {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Live drawing: the pull request

[Projector goes to sleep.]{.highlight-yellow} We draw this one by hand.

::: {.notes}
Live drawing 4 (pull request): complete the merge point from drawing 3, pause at the join for the human review, then the lines meet. Under 3 minutes. Fallback: images/drawing-4-pull-request.png.
:::

## My turn

<!-- Filled by issue #18: PR, review, merge demo -->

## Your turn

<!-- Filled by issue #18: learners open, review, and merge PRs in pairs -->

```{r}
#| echo: false
countdown(minutes = 15)
```

::: {.notes}
This block is protected. If the day is behind, B7 Publish is cut, never this. Bounded regroup: 2 minutes, then park-and-pair.
:::

# B7: Publish {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Our turn

<!-- Filled by issue #19: publish via GitHub Pages together -->

::: {.notes}
First block to cut. If running behind at 14:05, skip to the fallback slide and hand the time to B8.
:::

## Fallback: what publishing looks like

<!-- Filled by issue #19: pre-published example, steps in one breath -->

::: {.notes}
This slide stands alone for the cut case.
:::

# B8: Concept map revisit and wrap-up {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Your turn

<!-- Filled by issue #20: closing concept map, compare with the morning map -->

```{r}
#| echo: false
countdown(minutes = 8)
```

::: {.notes}
Hard start at 14:15: whatever block is running stops. B8 is protected and runs even on the worst day.
:::

## Wrap-up

<!-- Filled by issue #20: recap, side benefits, follow-up, feedback -->

## Thanks!

Slides created via revealjs and Quarto: <https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/>

Access slides as [PDF on GitHub](https://github.com/gitforsci-ghe/website/raw/main/workshop/slides.pdf)

All material is licensed under [Creative Commons Attribution Share Alike 4.0 International](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/).

## References
